description: Root category for all programming bugs

description: Bugs around return values and basic contract behavior
label: BugCategory
name: FunctionContractError

label: BugCategory

description: Pointer lifetime, dereference, and ownership mistakes
label: BugCategory
name: PointerLifetimeError

description: Modern C++ lifetime and move/ownership issues
label: BugCategory
name: ModernCppLifetimeError

description: Iterator invalidation and range misuse
label: BugCategory

description: Formatted I/O or output API misuse
label: BugCategory

name: SynchronizationError

description: Belief that loop boundaries include one more or one fewer valid iteration than they actually do.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandLoopBoundary

description: Belief that a loop progresses correctly even when state is not updated toward termination.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandLoopProgress

description: Incorrect mental model of initialization value or start state of a loop variable.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandLoopInitialization

description: Belief that an array of size n has valid last index n instead of n-1.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandLastValidIndex

description: Confusion between element position and element contents during traversal or update.
label: Misconception
name: ConfuseIndexWithValue

description: Belief that a pointer can be dereferenced safely without checking validity or lifetime.
label: Misconception
name: AssumePointerIsAlwaysValid

description: Belief that a pointer remains valid after free/delete.
label: Misconception
name: AssumeFreedPointerStillUsable

description: Belief that manually allocated memory is automatically released when forgotten.
label: Misconception
name: AssumeManualMemoryCleansItself

description: Belief that omitting a required return value has no observable consequence.
label: Misconception
name: AssumeMissingReturnIsBenign

description: Belief that recursion can terminate correctly without a precise base case.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandBaseCase

description: Belief that recursive calls need not reduce problem size toward the base case.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandRecursiveProgress

description: Belief that iterators remain valid after container modifications that invalidate them.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandIteratorInvalidation

description: Belief that an object is fully reusable after its resources were moved away.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandMovedFromState

description: Confusion about single ownership, shared ownership, and non-owning references.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandOwnershipModel

description: Belief that deleting through a base pointer is safe without a virtual destructor.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandPolymorphicDestruction

description: Belief that resource cleanup remains correct under exceptional control flow without RAII.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandExceptionSafety

description: Belief that any format specifier can print any type without mismatch problems.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandFormatSpecifierTypeMatch

description: Belief that waiting on conditions does not require disciplined locking and predicate checks.
label: Misconception
name: MisunderstandConditionWaitingProtocol

description: Program runs but produces wrong output for one or more cases.
label: Symptom
name: IncorrectOutput

description: Program does not terminate in expected time or gets stuck.
label: Symptom
name: ProgramHangs

description: Program exceeds time limit or performance budget.
label: Symptom
name: Timeout

description: Program aborts, segfaults, or terminates unexpectedly.
label: Symptom
name: Crash

description: Program accesses array or container outside valid range.
label: Symptom
name: OutOfBoundsAccess

description: Program dereferences a null pointer or invalid nullable handle.
label: Symptom
name: NullDereference

description: Program accesses memory after it has been released.
label: Symptom
name: UseAfterFreeSymptom

description: Memory usage grows because allocations are not released.
label: Symptom
name: UnboundedMemoryGrowth

description: Iterator or view is used after invalidation.
label: Symptom
name: IteratorInvalidAccess

description: A non-void function path finishes without returning a value.
label: Symptom
name: ControlReachesEndOfNonVoid

description: Same ownership resource is released multiple times.
label: Symptom
name: DoubleReleaseSymptom

description: Output or state varies across runs due to synchronization mistakes.
label: Symptom
name: NonDeterministicBehavior

description: Program violates problem specification and returns incorrect results.
label: Effect
name: WrongAnswer

description: Execution enters undefined behavior with unstable or unsafe outcomes.
label: Effect
name: UndefinedBehavior

description: Program consumes memory or CPU excessively.
label: Effect
name: ResourceExhaustion

description: Bug may create memory corruption or exploitable behavior.
label: Effect
name: SecurityRisk

description: Program never reaches a terminating state for some inputs.
label: Effect
name: NonTermination

description: Program corrupts values or object state in memory.
label: Effect
name: DataCorruption

description: Program leaves resources unreleased under some control paths.
label: Effect
name: PartialCleanup

description: Program violates expected library or language usage contract.
label: Effect
name: APIContractViolation

description: Replace incorrect boundary condition with the correct inclusive/exclusive condition.
label: FixStrategy
name: AdjustLoopBoundary

description: Update loop state so each iteration moves toward termination.
label: FixStrategy
name: EnsureLoopProgress

description: Initialize loop variable from the intended starting state.
label: FixStrategy
name: CorrectLoopInitialization

description: Constrain index to valid range and use length-based reasoning.
label: FixStrategy
name: ValidateArrayBoundary

description: Use distinct variables or logic for index and element value.
label: FixStrategy
name: SeparateIndexAndValueRoles

description: Check for null or impossible state before dereferencing, or redesign ownership.
label: FixStrategy
name: CheckPointerBeforeDereference

description: Ensure each allocated resource has one matching release.
label: FixStrategy
name: ReleaseExactlyOnce

description: Clear or stop using handles after free/delete or hand over ownership safely.
label: FixStrategy
name: InvalidateAfterRelease

description: Tie resource lifetime to object lifetime using RAII wrappers and automatic cleanup.
label: FixStrategy
name: AdoptRAII

description: Replace raw owning pointers with std::unique_ptr or std::shared_ptr as appropriate.
label: FixStrategy
name: UseSmartPointer

description: Add explicit valid return statements on every control path of a non-void function.
label: FixStrategy
name: ReturnOnAllPaths

description: Add a precise recursive base case that stops recursion.
label: FixStrategy
name: DefineBaseCase

description: Ensure each recursive call reduces input toward the base case.
label: FixStrategy
name: ReduceProblemSize

description: Reacquire iterator/reference after container operations that may invalidate it.
label: FixStrategy
name: RefreshIteratorAfterModification

description: Use moved-from objects only in documented valid ways or reinitialize them.
label: FixStrategy
name: DoNotDependOnMovedFromValue

description: Make base destructor virtual when deleting derived objects via base pointers.
label: FixStrategy
name: AddVirtualDestructor

description: Use the correct format specifier for the actual argument type.
label: FixStrategy
name: MatchFormatSpecifierToType

description: Protect shared state with locks and wait in a predicate-based loop.
label: FixStrategy
name: UsePredicateWaitPattern

description: Loop or indexing logic uses a boundary that is one too small or one too large.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: OffByOneError

description: Loop exit condition is unreachable or loop state never progresses to termination.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: InfiniteLoopError

description: Loop variable or progress state is not updated correctly inside or by the loop header.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: MissingLoopUpdateError

description: Loop starts from an incorrect initial state or index.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: WrongLoopInitializationError

description: Loop condition expresses the wrong continuation rule.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: WrongLoopConditionError

description: Built-in array is indexed outside its valid bounds.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: ArrayOutOfBoundsError

description: Logic confuses array index with array element value.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: IndexValueConfusionError

description: Null or otherwise invalid pointer/reference-like state is dereferenced.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: NullDereferenceError

description: Dynamically allocated memory is not released when no longer needed.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: MemoryLeakError

description: Memory is accessed after the corresponding allocation was released.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: UseAfterFreeError

description: The same allocation or ownership resource is released more than once.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: DoubleFreeError

description: Pointer continues to reference storage whose lifetime has ended.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: DanglingPointerError

description: Variable or object state is read before receiving a valid initialization.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: UninitializedValueUsageError

description: A non-void function can complete without returning a value.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: MissingReturnValueError

description: Recursive algorithm uses an incorrect or unreachable base case.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: WrongRecursionBaseCaseError

description: Recursive calls do not reduce problem size toward the base case.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: NoRecursiveProgressError

description: Code uses iterators, references, or views after container modification invalidates them.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: IteratorInvalidationError

description: Code relies on moved-from object state as if it were unchanged.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: UseAfterMoveError

description: Deleting a derived object through a base pointer without a virtual destructor.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: MissingVirtualDestructorError

description: Custom resource-owning type defines inconsistent copy/move/destructor behavior.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: RuleOfFiveViolation

description: Resource cleanup or object invariants break under exception paths.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: ExceptionSafetyViolation

description: std::vector access goes outside valid logical range.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: VectorOutOfRangeError

description: Formatted I/O uses a specifier that does not match argument type.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: FormatSpecifierMismatchError

description: Wait/notify is used without proper locking or predicate-based rechecking.
label: ProgrammingBug
name: ConditionVariableMisuseError

description: Memory usage grows due to leaks or incomplete destruction.
label: Effect
name: MemoryGrowth
